methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Apis Cor is a seed-stage construction technology company that has raised $5.5M to date. The Housing & Construction sector comprises a narrow peer universe of two private comparable companies (ICON and BotBuilt) and no public traded peers, limiting the applicability of traditional multiples-based approaches.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised ($5.5M) as a proxy for implicit seed valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-Series A advancement yields $13.75M. This reflects the expected step-up in valuation upon progression to the next funding round, adjusted for the capital intensity and execution risk inherent in construction technology.

The peer-median approach is not applicable given the absence of at least three comparables meeting stage, sector, and tier filters. Relying on sector-wide medians at this company density would introduce material bias rather than reduce uncertainty.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $12M (low) to $16M (median $14M), centered on the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include execution velocity on product-market fit, regulatory pathway clarity for on-site deployment, and capital requirements to reach Series A milestones. The narrow comparable universe limits confidence in range bounds; follow-on valuation credibility will depend on traction metrics in coming quarters.
